question: Is the image a realistic macro photograph taken from an overhead shot at a slight angle?
Is an open pocket watch movement placed at the center of the frame on a soft cushion?
Is the movement metal a mix of silver and gold with clearly detailed gears and plates?
Is the pocket watch case lid flipped open facing toward the top of the frame?
Is the inner side of the case lid mirror-polished metal reflecting lamp light points?
Are there exactly two round watch dials placed side by side to the right of the movement?
Is one watch dial white and the other matte black?
Are both watch dials completely blank (no indices and no hands)?
Are there exactly two precision screwdrivers placed horizontally above the dials?
Does the screwdriver with the dark blue handle have its tip pointing toward the left?
Does the screwdriver with the dark red handle have its tip pointing toward the right?
Is there a precision tweezer placed diagonally below the screwdrivers with its tip pointing toward the bottom?
Does a circular magnifying lens with a white frame enter the frame halfway from the left side?
Does the magnifying lens project a circular bright spot that magnifies the gear textures on the upper left of the movement?
Is there a compartmentalized metal parts tray at the lower left of the frame?
Does the parts tray contain tiny screws and springs?
Does the parts tray contain exactly seven ruby jewel bearings with transparent red color?
Does the parts tray contain exactly one gray gear with obvious edge refraction?
Is there a black oiler pen lying to the lower right of the parts tray?
Is the oiler pen's metal tip pointing toward the right of the frame?
Is there a rubber air blower standing upright beside the oiler pen with its nozzle facing upper left?
Is there a transparent ultrasonic cleaning tank in the upper right of the frame?
Is the cleaning tank's lid closed and the power indicator light off?
Is there a light gray chamois cloth with slight creases placed in front of the cleaning tank?
Is there a metal caliper lying flat at the far right side of the frame?
Is the caliper's scale area a smooth metal surface without any markings?
Is there a timing machine device below the caliper?
Is the timing machine's screen completely black with no display?
Is there a small round glass dome placed inverted at the lower right corner of the frame?
Is the glass dome empty inside?
